source_display: AI as Brain Crutch for ADHD
evidence: I've always been so interested in building out stuff that, you know, essentially tries to act as a, like, crutch. Like a, like, essentially the equivalent of like a brain crutch for someone with ADHD.
avoid the 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is.
A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options—whether to scale, attract others, or seek investment.
He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly.
Vrijen outlined a spectrum of community approaches: one-to-many broadcasting → platforms with comment sections → private user research groups → full communities.
A peer-to-peer strategic conversation between Vrijen Attawar (founder of Careerspan) and Chris Hanson (special education teacher turned edtech founder)
Chris noted his brand's tagline 'inspired by people like you, created by people like you' and his desire to stay involved in sales.
My drive for authenticity is part of the reason why I don't go on social media so much is just like. Feels like it's all. Feels like I'd have to be disingenuous.
Chris is building an ADHD support product called '360.' The discussion evolved into a candid exchange about product strategy, go-to-market challenges.
Positioning: Careerspan as a 'human data company'. Video demo should be 'human data oriented' — show the data collection, then the results.
Logan's insight: 'Why don't we make a 30-second look at the information we capture via Careerspan?' Shift from explaining to showing — visual demonstration over word-heavy explanations.
Reference to Eightfold lawsuit — positioning against publicly scraped data risks. Hook: 'This moment in time is important' (regulatory pressure on third-party data).
Key differentiator: opted-in, first-party candidate data. Core message: 'We are a great solution because we collect first-party data'
Logan: 'A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action'. Positioning first-party data as core message for outreach.
Two distinct video/message versions: 1. Sourcing/Matching/External Hiring — for recruiting teams. 2. Internal Mobility — for enterprise HR/L&D
Decision: 45-60 second demo video, not a deck. Logan: 'A deck is not as impactful as watching it in action'
Springboard's 'baller move': including workforce board lead list upfront (giving value immediately). Careerspan's materials require too much explanation — need to lead with data value.
Reviewed an email from Springboard as a reference... Problem identified: Careerspan's existing materials require too much explanation. Video demo should be 'human data oriented'.
